22c86f758100.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
22c86f758100.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
22c86f758100.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

8f0f71e4e963.title: Close the hygiene gap — 4 to 5
8f0f71e4e963.description: An FSA rating of 4 is visible on your Google listing and the FSA website. Customers see it before they see your menu. Address the inspector's specific findings.
8f0f71e4e963.expected_upside: Remove the trust ceiling on your overall score

37c24fa2404f.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
37c24fa2404f.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

5be8864100d3.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
5be8864100d3.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
5be8864100d3.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

10ad30e3b970.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
10ad30e3b970.description: Your 4.7/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P11.1). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
10ad30e3b970.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

c45e2ee536ae.title: Ranked #9 of 10 locally — peers are ahead
c45e2ee536ae.description: At percentile 11.1, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

3726ad9344a2.title: Guest experience trailing — investigate what peers do differently
3726ad9344a2.description: You trail peers by 1.4 points on experience. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

d30887668371.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
d30887668371.description: At 120 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
